statement.normalized: Let $G$ be a graph on at most $\aleph_1$ vertices which contains no $K_4$ and no $K_{\aleph_0,\aleph_0}$ (the complete bipartite graph with $\aleph_0$ vertices in each class). Is it true that\[\omega_1^2 \to (\omega_1\omega, G)^2?\]What about finite $G$?
statement.original: Let $G$ be a graph on at most $\aleph_1$ vertices which contains no $K_4$ and no $K_{\aleph_0,\aleph_0}$ (the complete bipartite graph with $\aleph_0$ vertices in each class). Is it true that\[\omega_1^2 \to (\omega_1\omega, G)^2?\]What about finite $G$?
